task_completion.description: Navigate the view to the yard or driveway area of the sample house project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D or 3D workspace view should be focused on the exterior yard or driveway area of the house, preparing for construction.

task_completion.description: Place at least four vertical posts to support the carport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least four distinct vertical posts or columns (created using the Post/Column tool) should be visible in the yard or driveway area, arranged in a structural layout.

task_completion.description: Ensure the structure is an open-sided shelter without enclosing walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The space between the vertical posts must be open. There should be no walls connecting the posts that would make it an enclosed garage.

task_completion.description: Add a roof over the vertical posts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A roof structure (created using the Roof tool) must be visible covering the area defined by the vertical posts.
A 'Save As' dialog box showing the filename 'Carport_Design' (or 'Carport_Design.ddp'), or the main application window title bar displaying 'Carport_Design' after saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the DreamPlan GUI to construct the carport.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking on tools (like Post/Column and Roof) and interacting with the 2D/3D workspace, rather than using a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the carport was actually built within the software's engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The carport should appear as native 3D/2D objects within the DreamPlan interface, matching the rendering style of the sample house, not as an imported flat image.

integrity.description: Verify the project was saved via the application menu.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save operation should be performed through the DreamPlan 'File > Save As' menu or save icon, not by using terminal commands to rename or copy files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: DreamPlan Home Design Software uses the Post/Column tool for vertical supports and the Roof tool for roofing. The task requires an open-sided structure, meaning no walls should enclose the posts. Note: The native project file extension for DreamPlan is .ddp. The agent should save the file as 'Carport_Design.ddp', even if task metadata incorrectly suggests a '.dplan' extension.
